verses.text: David calls the mighty to ascribe glory and strength to the LORD and to worship Him 'in the beauty of holiness.' Heaven and earth owe Him praise. True worship bows before His holiness as well as His power.

David calls the mighty to ascribe glory and strength to the LORD and to worship Him 'in the beauty of holiness.' Heaven and earth owe Him praise. True worship bows before His holiness as well as His power.
'The voice of the LORD is upon the waters'; it is powerful and full of majesty. The thunder is His voice. The storm that frightens men is but the speech of their God.
'The voice of the LORD is upon the waters'; it is powerful and full of majesty. The thunder is His voice. The storm that frightens men is but the speech of their God.
That voice breaks the mighty cedars and divides the flames of fire. Nothing stands before it. The strongest things of earth are splintered by a word from heaven.
That voice breaks the mighty cedars and divides the flames of fire. Nothing stands before it. The strongest things of earth are splintered by a word from heaven.
That voice breaks the mighty cedars and divides the flames of fire. Nothing stands before it. The strongest things of earth are splintered by a word from heaven.
It shakes the wilderness and makes the hinds calve, while in His temple all cry, 'Glory.' Creation trembles and worship rises. The same voice that shakes the earth gathers His people to praise.
It shakes the wilderness and makes the hinds calve, while in His temple all cry, 'Glory.' Creation trembles and worship rises. The same voice that shakes the earth gathers His people to praise.

'The LORD sitteth upon the flood'; He reigns as King for ever and gives strength and peace to His people. He rules the raging waters. The God enthroned above every storm grants calm to those who are His.

'The LORD sitteth upon the flood'; He reigns as King for ever and gives strength and peace to His people. He rules the raging waters. The God enthroned above every storm grants calm to those who are His.
